projectDescription: A lightweight SDL2-based graphical launcher for Super Mario 64: Coop Deluxe on Linux, written in Rust, with mod management, DynOS pack support, profile management, network configuration, and a built-in mod download browser.
keyFeatures: 5-tier game binary path resolution (CLI arg → env var → profile override → launcher.toml → auto-detect)
SDL2 graphical UI with splash screen, animated vinyl disc, and background music playback
Mod Manager: enable/disable Lua mods and folder-based mods stored in ~/.local/share/sm64coopdx/mods/
DynOS Packs Manager: toggle DynOS resource packs from ~/.local/share/sm64coopdx/dynos/packs/
Profile Manager: create, rename, delete, and activate named profiles each with independent game config
Network Configuration: Local, Client, Server, and CoopNet modes with per-profile settings
Built-in Download Browser: browse and install 1,000+ mods from the bundled database JSON
ROM validation via MD5 checksum (20b854b239203baf6c961b850a4a51a2) with automatic copy to savepath
Gamepad support with configurable button-to-action mapping and B-button hold-to-exit
Clean game environment spawning with env_clear() + whitelist to prevent library conflicts
publicApiSurface: --game-path <PATH>  (CLI flag to override game binary location)
SM64COOPDX_PATH     (environment variable for game binary path)
game::build_all_game_args(mods, network, profile, profile_dir, data_dir) -> Vec<String>
managers::profile_manager::ProfileConfig (playername, skip_intro, no_discord, fullscreen, headless, game_path, ...)
